stubs_used: Semgrep findings — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md with one Open=false finding stub; no real Semgrep run.
Phase 3 SAST — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_semgrep_pass.json instead of running Semgrep.
Phase 3 accessibility — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_lighthouse_accessibility.json instead of running Lighthouse.
Phase 3 performance — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_lighthouse_performance.json instead of running Lighthouse.
GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other; push failed (expected, no real network).
TypeScript source/test — only stub `// stub` files in src/ and tests/; no npm/pnpm/jest run.
Phase advancement — bumped current_phase manually via jq between every phase (check-phase-gate.sh is consistency-only — does not advance current_phase). Recorded as expectation mismatch.

Pre-commit gate (BL-006/BL-015) — fed JSON envelopes on stdin to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh to simulate the Claude Code PreToolUse path; the hook is registered in .claude/settings.json PreToolUse[Bash].

Phase 2 init verification — auto-marked branch_protection_configured, project_scaffolded, data_model_applied because the verifier expects a real lockfile and a host manifest, neither of which exist in stub mode.
init_driver: Heredoc piped to bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh with answers (in order): project name uat-agent-9, description, platform=4 (web), track=3 (full), deployment=1 (personal), Full+personal warning confirm=y, language=7 (typescript), project_dir, Continue=y, Proceed-with-plan=y, host=4 (other), visibility=1 (private), https://example.com/fake.git, push attest=yes. init.sh exited 1 because create_and_protect_remote() runs `git push` against the fake URL and `set -euo pipefail` aborts the script after project creation. Project skeleton on disk is otherwise complete and usable for downstream tests.

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exit code = 1. Same root cause as agent-1: create_and_protect_remote() runs `git push` against the fake remote which fails; with `set -euo pipefail` the failure aborts main() after project creation but before verify-install/print_next_steps. The on-disk project is otherwise complete.

tests.details: settings.json Stop hooks include `.claude/framework/hooks/stop-checklist.sh` (CDF) and `scripts/session-end-qdrant-reminder.sh` (Solo).

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers to bash init.sh. Only --dry-run and --help flags exist. Prompt count and ordering vary with installed-tool state (Docker check, host-aware repo prompts after project creation, Full+personal warning interjects between TRACK and LANGUAGE only when those values combine).
framework_bugs.expected: A --non-interactive / --answers-from FILE flag so UAT and CI can drive init.sh deterministically.
framework_bugs.actual: First attempt deadlocked in an infinite `Invalid choice` loop because the Full+personal confirmation prompt interleaves between deployment and language and shifted the heredoc's answer-to-prompt alignment. Fixed by re-ordering, but no documentation of the exact prompt sequence exists.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh exits non-zero on documented happy path when remote push fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh selecting host=other and provide https://example.com/fake.git as the remote URL (per runbook Setup constraint). `git push` to fake.git fails; create_and_protect_remote returns 1; `set -euo pipefail` aborts main() before the verify-install/print_next_steps tail. Exit code = 1.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh tolerates a remote-push failure, leaves remediation guidance, and finishes the create_project tail (verify-install + next-steps); or exits with a documented `[BLOCKED at host-aware repo creation — fix and rerun scripts/check-gate.sh --repair]` code.
framework_bugs.actual: Bare `[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials` then immediate non-zero exit. verify-install/print_next_steps are skipped. Same finding as agent-1 — confirms reproducibility on a different platform/language/track combination (web/typescript/full).

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh advertises only --dry-run and --help; the runbook still documents flags like --non-interactive that do not exist.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh main() flag parser vs RUNBOOK.md Section A example.

documentation_gaps.gap: Number/order of init.sh prompts is undocumented and varies with branch state (Full+personal warning, host=other branch, push attest). UAT agents have no canonical prompt list.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no prompt-sequence doc in docs/.

documentation_gaps.gap: Track 'full' for personal deployments triggers an extra y/N confirmation prompt that shifts heredoc alignment by one. Should be documented in the runbook for UAT drivers.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh ~330 (Full+personal warning) vs RUNBOOK.md Section A 'Init driver' subsection.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 exited 0; .phase1_architecture.started_at populated.

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: jq '.phase1_architecture.steps_completed' returned [].

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed in order with exit 0. Phase 1 has no per-step artifact gate.

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 with `Phase gates consistent.` HOWEVER it is a consistency check, not a phase advancer — it does not update .phase-state.json.current_phase.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: current_phase did NOT auto-advance after the gate. Same finding as agent-1: no script in scripts/ does this advancement. Bumped manually via jq.

framework_bugs.summary: No script advances current_phase between phases
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all 5 phase1_architecture steps; run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; observe .claude/phase-state.json `.current_phase` is still 1 (or 0 — depends on prior manual edits). No `--advance` or `--approve-gate` subcommand on check-phase-gate.sh, check-gate.sh, or process-checklist.sh exists that flips current_phase.
framework_bugs.expected: A documented script (e.g., scripts/process-checklist.sh --advance-phase) that, after gate consistency + approval-log entry, atomically writes the new current_phase value.
framework_bugs.actual: Phase advancement is left to manual jq editing. The runbook asserts phase_state_advances_to_2 as a test case, so the framework's phase model and the runbook's protocol are out of sync. Confirms agent-1 finding on a different platform/track.

documentation_gaps.gap: Builders Guide / RUNBOOK should explicitly name the script (or document the manual procedure) that bumps current_phase after a gate passes.
documentation_gaps.where: docs/reference/builders-guide.md (Phase Transitions); RUNBOOK.md Section B last row.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1, .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1' and .build_loop.started_at populated.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: Sequential complete-step calls (tests_written → tests_verified_failing → implemented → security_audit → documentation_updated → feature_recorded) all succeeded with stub artifacts. The build_loop:security_audit gate accepted docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 mandatory build-loop steps were complete (the .git/hooks/pre-commit ran gitleaks + Semgrep + TDD ordering on the staged stub TS files).

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: When build_loop slot was cleared (jq '.build_loop = {}'), simulated PreToolUse envelope `{"command":"git commit --allow-empty -m \"feat(bl006-trigger): no loop\""}` fed to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh returned the rich BL-006 deny: `'feat(...)' commit blocked — no Build Loop active. MVP Cutline work and all features require a Build Loop`. Confirmed BL-006 wiring. PARTIAL CAVEAT (re-confirms agent-1): the same `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` invoked **directly via shell** (bypassing the Claude Code PreToolUse hook) succeeded with exit 0. BL-006 enforcement lives only in scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh (PreToolUse), NOT in .git/hooks/pre-commit. Direct shell commits bypass it.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote sentinel via `scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer ... --options 'A1: option A' 'A2: option B' --recommendation A1`. Hook denied a chore commit with the rich `Pending question:` message. NOTE: the help text says `--options "A1: ..." "A2: ..."` (separate args) — runbook example `--options A,B` does not work.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent simulated chore commit through the hook returned EXIT=0 with no deny output.

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: test-gate.sh --record-feature uat-feat-1 succeeded; .claude/build-progress.json `.features_completed` = ['uat-feat-1'].

framework_bugs.summary: BL-006 only enforced in Claude Code PreToolUse — direct shell git commits bypass it
framework_bugs.reproduction: In a project with no active build_loop, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): bypass'` directly in shell. Commit succeeds. The Solo enforcement lives in scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh (registered as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook), not in .git/hooks/pre-commit. .git/hooks/pre-commit only runs gitleaks + Semgrep + TDD ordering + schema migration — no BL-006 / build-loop check.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should also be enforced in .git/hooks/pre-commit so humans, CI, and any non-Claude-Code commit path cannot bypass it. (Or, if BL-006 is intentionally Claude-Code-only, the framework docs should say so loudly.)
framework_bugs.actual: BL-006 is enforced only when Claude Code is the commit author. Any human running `git commit` from terminal completely bypasses the build-loop requirement. Confirms agent-1 finding on web/typescript/full.

framework_bugs.summary: Phase 2 init verifier silently demands a host manifest and lockfile that fake-remote stub projects cannot provide
framework_bugs.reproduction: After init.sh with host=other + fake URL, run `scripts/process-checklist.sh --verify-init`. Output: `[FAIL] branch_protection_configured — host dispatcher or manifest missing` and `[FAIL] project_scaffolded — no lockfile found`. The pre-commit-gate then refuses every git commit with `[FAIL] Phase 2 initialization not verified`.
framework_bugs.expected: Either (a) init.sh writes a host manifest for host=other so verify-init can complete; or (b) verify-init's failure modes link to the exact remediation script (e.g., `scripts/check-gate.sh --backfill-host`); or (c) a `--mark-verified` escape hatch for stub/UAT scenarios.
framework_bugs.actual: Verify-init auto-marks `data_model_applied` etc. silently after a second run (the first run says "Cannot auto-verify" but never marks; the second run does mark them as `previously marked complete`). Confusing UX — a single `--verify-init` run should be idempotent.

documentation_gaps.gap: Pending-approval sentinel CLI surface differs from runbook example. Runbook says `--options A,B` (comma-separated string); script requires separate args like `--options 'A1: option A' 'A2: option B'`. Builders Guide should publish the canonical CLI invocation.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section C bl015 row vs scripts/pending-approval.sh --help.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 trigger test in runbook ('attempt feat: commit with no loop') only fires when build_loop slot is empty `{}`. After --start-feature + complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, the feature stays in build_loop and BL-006 considers the loop satisfied. Runbook should specify whether the test expects the loop to be cleared first.
Split-enforcement story (.git/hooks/pre-commit for secrets/SAST/TDD vs scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh PreToolUse for BL-006/BL-015/phase gates) is not documented end-to-end.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT steps (agents_dispatched → template_generated → orchestrator_notified → results_received → completeness_verified → bugs_consolidated → triage_complete → remediation_complete → gate_passed) completed sequentially with exit 0. No artifact gate on UAT steps.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 phase3_validation steps (integration_testing through legal_review) completed with exit 0. Artifact gates fired correctly: integration_testing satisfied by tests/ dir, security_hardening by stub semgrep_pass.json, accessibility/performance by stub Lighthouse files, legal_review printed INFO message and proceeded.

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh has no --help, no --default-template flag, and no fixture in templates/uat/ that lints clean.

phase3_validation artifact gates (integration_testing, security_hardening, accessibility_audit, performance_audit, results_archived, legal_review) are documented only inline in process-checklist.sh. There is no consolidated `Phase 3 Required Artifacts` checklist for agents.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/process-checklist.sh; docs/reference/builders-guide.md should publish the artifact-gate matrix.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 exits 0; .phase4_release.started_at populated.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 phase4_release steps completed: production_build (no artifact gate), rollback_tested (gated on docs/test-results/*rollback*; satisfied by stub), go_live_verified (gated on RELEASE_NOTES.md; satisfied — init.sh creates that file), monitoring_configured (gated on HANDOFF.md mentioning monitoring/sentry; satisfied after HANDOFF.md added with Sentry mention), handoff_written (gated on HANDOFF.md), handoff_tested (gated on docs/test-results/*handoff*).

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: Pre-creating HANDOFF.md with monitoring keywords satisfied both monitoring_configured and handoff_written gates. (Without HANDOFF.md, agent-1 reported the gate fires with exit 1 and the message `HANDOFF.md not found — monitoring configuration should be documented there.`)

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: phase4_release:go_live_verified gates on RELEASE_NOTES.md existence. init.sh ships a stub RELEASE_NOTES.md so the gate passed without manual intervention.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .phase4_release.steps_completed has all 6 entries; .phase4_release.step = 6. .phase-state.json .current_phase = 4 (manually bumped). NOTE: there is no `released` state — current_phase remains numeric 4 after all steps complete. Runbook should clarify whether 4 is terminal.

documentation_gaps.gap: Phase 4 terminal state ambiguity — runbook says `.current_phase` should equal 4 OR 'released'. The framework only ever uses numeric 0-4; there is no `released` state. Choose one and document.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section E phase4_completion_state row; docs/reference/builders-guide.md Phase 4 description.

documentation_gaps.gap: Phase 4 artifact gates (HANDOFF.md required for monitoring_configured AND handoff_written; RELEASE_NOTES.md required for go_live_verified; rollback test file in docs/test-results) are scattered in process-checklist.sh. Builders Guide should publish a Phase 4 artifact checklist.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: gitleaks git --no-banner returns `no leaks found` over 2 commits / ~1.17MB. (Naive `grep -E 'password|api[_-]?key|secret|token'` over docs has false positives; runbook should clarify it means real secret detection via gitleaks.)

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log does NOT exist after a normal happy-path run. Audit-log writes ONLY happen on (a) SOIF_FORCE_STEP bypass and (b) --reset-process subcommands. No FORCE/reset events occurred — file is correctly absent.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results.

overall_pass_reason: Three High-severity bugs reproduced (all also reported by agent-1, now confirmed on a different platform/language/track combo — base/personal/web/full/typescript): (1) init.sh exits non-zero on the documented happy-path host=other + fake URL flow because `git push` to the fake remote propagates through `set -euo pipefail`; (2) BL-006 build-loop enforcement only fires in Claude Code's PreToolUse path — direct shell `git commit -m 'feat(...)'` bypasses it entirely; (3) no script advances `current_phase` between phases, so the runbook's phase_state_advances_to_2 assertion can only be satisfied by manual jq editing. One Medium bug also recorded around the Phase 2 init verifier's host-manifest/lockfile expectations vs stub UAT scenarios. init.sh prompt-driver fragility re-confirmed (Full+personal warning prompt interleaves and shifted my heredoc alignment on first attempt).
summary: Base / personal / web / full / typescript end-to-end: project skeleton creation works, all phase 1-4 process steps complete with stub artifacts, BL-006 + BL-015 enforce correctly through the Claude Code PreToolUse hook, but init.sh exits 1 on the documented `host=other + fake URL` path, BL-006 has zero enforcement on direct shell commits, and the framework has no script that advances current_phase between phases — agents must edit phase-state.json manually. All three Highs reproduce findings from agent-1, confirming they are not platform/language/track-specific.
